---
title: "Failure to download extra data files"
date: 2013-06-16
forum: General Help
---

### Post by Ol'manScratch on 2013-06-16
Hi
I just got this message and I have never seen it before and because of the site I was browsing when it popped up, I'm curious about it.

[B]                                           "Failure to download extra data files

The following packages requested additional data downloads after package  installation, but the data could not be downloaded or could not be  processed.

ttf-mscorefonts-installer, flashplugin-installer

The download will be attempted again later, or you can try the download  again now.  Running this command requires an active Internet connection."                      
[/B]
I've been using Ubuntu for a couple of years, but I have not delved into it beyond what I need to know for the purposes I use. I updated my system using the Update Manager on 06/15/2013 and everything went well. I also cleared all the cookies from my system. I use "Ghostery" and "Do Not Track Me" Today I was browsing stories about the national intelligence leaks, and clicked over to the Director of National Intelligence site. read something and clicked away. Then I wondered if the site had left a cookie or other tracker. When I checked, there were several new cookies. Many were from sites I had not been to since clearing all the older cookies. None looked as if there was a national intelligence connection, but that's when I got the failure to download message above. I did not install it. I went back to the Update Manager, ran a check and there we no packages to install.

I have never seen this type of message before, but due to all of the recent spying stuff coming out, I'd like to know why I got the message and could it have had anything scary in it. I'm probably crazy as a loon to worry about this, but I consider being ultra cautious and curious to be a virtue.

I'm sorry to be so verbose with this, but I want to be clear on what I found. Thanks for any info you can offer.

Here is a screen shot of the cookies I found:
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]


[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]

### Post by Bashing-om on 2013-06-16
[COLOR=#000000]Ol'manScratch; Hi !

In this instance I do not think there is malicious intent in that both [/COLOR]**ttf-mscorefonts-installer, flashplugin-installer**
are installed packages on your system and the web page is looking for fonts and flash.
Flash, as i understand it... not much we can do about it anymore....  as flash is no longer under development for linux;
mscorefonts is a part of the "ubuntu-restricted-extras" package, that you may or may not want on your system.
If you want to pursue this and install the recommends, please use the ubuntu assets (package manager) to resolve the issue.
Do not rely on a outside source.

[INDENT][INDENT]just my thought, others may have variance[/INDENT][/INDENT]

---

### Post by Cheesemill on 2013-06-16
Nothing scary at all, it just looks as if the was a problem with your internet connection the first time you tried to install those packages.

The ttf-mscorefonts-installer and flashplugin-installer packages both need to download extra data when you install them as their licensing terms don't allow the data to be hosted on the Ubuntu servers. The first one downloads fonts from a Microsoft server, the second downloads the flash plugin from Adobe.

Try the following command to reinstall the packages...
```
sudo apt-get install --reinstall ttf-mscorefonts-installer flashplugin-installer
```

If a screen appears asking you to accept the Microsoft font license just hit TAB until the OK box is highlighted and then hit ENTER.

---
